content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN. Can the array be rearranged such that all the (non-empty) prefixes of the array has an odd sum?

Formally, does there exist a rearrangement array BBB of the array AAA such that B1+B2+‚Ä¶+BiB_1 + B_2 + \ldots + B_iB1‚Äã+B2‚Äã+‚Ä¶+Bi‚Äã is odd for every 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N. Output Yes\text{Yes}Yes if it is possible and No\text{No}No otherwise.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output Yes\text{Yes}Yes if it is possible to rearrange the array AAA to make all prefix sums be odd, and No\text{No}No otherwise.


It is allowed to print each character in either case, for example, YES\text{YES}YES, yes\text{yes}yes and yEs\text{yEs}yEs will all be accepted.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The given array AAA is already good because the only prefix sum is 111, which is odd.
Test Case 2 : The given array AAA has 222 even prefix sums. There exists no good rearrangement of the array.
Test Case 3 : We can rearrange to [1,2,2][1, 2, 2][1,2,2] which has prefix sums 1,3,51, 3, 51,3,5, all of which are odd.
